title: betula mandshurica var. japonica
intro: Betula mandshurica var. japonica, also known as Korean white birch, is a deciduous tree species native to East Asia, characterized by its white bark and small leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which are attributed to the presence of salicin and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Korean white birch include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and improve cardiovascular health due to its ability to lower cholesterol levels and blood pressure. Korean white birch is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
